input.command:
git add nix/module.nix && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Fix NixOS module YAML: nest telegram fields under chat key

The lib.optionalString for telegram config inserted a blank line that
broke YAML nesting, causing telegram_chat_id and telegram_allowed_users
to appear as top-level keys instead of under chat:.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

input.command:
gh pr create --title "Fix NixOS module YAML: nest telegram fields under chat key" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary
- Removed blank line in `lib.optionalString` for telegram config in `nix/module.nix` that broke YAML nesting
- `telegram_chat_id` and `telegram_allowed_users` are now correctly nested under the `chat:` key instead of appearing at the top level

## Test plan
- [ ] Build NixOS module with `chatBackend = "telegram"` and verify generated YAML has telegram fields under `chat:`
- [ ] Build with `chatBackend = "googlechat"` and verify no extra blank lines
- [x] `go test ./...` passes

Run: 20260401-2132-7725
Fixes #12

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)"

content.text:
PR created: https://github.com/patflynn/reel-life/pull/13

The fix removes the blank line after the `''` opening of `lib.optionalString` in `nix/module.nix:166-168`. That blank line was breaking the YAML mapping context, causing `telegram_chat_id` and `telegram_allowed_users` to be parsed as top-level keys instead of staying nested under `chat:`.

result:
PR created: https://github.com/patflynn/reel-life/pull/13

The fix removes the blank line after the `''` opening of `lib.optionalString` in `nix/module.nix:166-168`. That blank line was breaking the YAML mapping context, causing `telegram_chat_id` and `telegram_allowed_users` to be parsed as top-level keys instead of staying nested under `chat:`.
